issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.wired.com/account/profile/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wired.com%2Faccount%2Fprofile%2F) ### Comment > Parts of wired.com do not work correctly with AdGuard enabled. For this specific case, the account profile page is missing almost all of its content. Wired.com appears to be unable to get the user's subscription status when AdGuard is turned on. (This affects other pages as well.) ``` Username: @kaiyoma ``` ### Screenshots

comment.body:
> [@kaiyoma](https://github.com/kaiyoma) Was tracking protection disabled during the check? Not sure if we can help without a subscription. With free account I can't read that article even without AdGuard.

I had disabled all filters, but left the "big button" turned on. That was still causing interference with the operation of the website.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

github so weird but anyway
this website makes the user go into their network tab of a roblox game they want, then paste some "random" code that casually contains their roblo-security cookie code, letting the user log in to the victims account (holy big words) but anyways it just steals the users cookie info, and im pretty sure if it sees something like this in the input: _|WARNING:-DO-NOT-SHARE-THIS--(blahblahblah) it will tell the user to wait, while it just steals their cookies.
sorry idk how to explain too well, but i think that https://bloxtools.nl/tools/Game and bloxtools.nl (other most likely scam services) is a malware scam thingamajig.

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

issue.title: www.mako.co.il

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.mako.co.il/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mako.co.il%2F) ### Comment > This website has initial popup ad that you need to click on close in order to see the website content. > When I enable the ad block, I don’t see the initial ad and, not the close button. > What left is just empty gray website (just the navbar is shown). > I tried to whitelisting this website, but it’s still not working. > In order for the website to work I need to disable the ad blocker general toggle (in ad blocker app) ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/bdoxeb79stcg0ccgcwwc8kwo4gcgsss32krcw9z174.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/sop4ungxwfksg8gckcko0gsogocc80o9tni9lbh9ek.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/t1tr2wz4busksswo4cw4scsok0gwgskbm9lyx22dc8.png?nc=1)
### System configuration Information | Value --- | --- AdGuard product: | AdGuard for iOS v4.5.15 License type: | paid Advanced protection: | enabled DNS filtering: | server: `Just enabled the dns option inside the app its default settings`

AdGuard Base,

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.mako.co.il/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mako.co.il%2F) ### Comment > This website has initial popup ad that you need to click on close in order to see the website content. > When I enable the ad block, I don’t see the initial ad and, not the close button. > What left is just empty gray website (just the navbar is shown). > I tried to whitelisting this website, but it’s still not working. > In order for the website to work I need to disable the ad blocker general toggle (in ad blocker app) ### Screenshots

_No response_

### Add your comment and screenshots

2. <comment>
2. Screenshots
<details><summary>Screenshot 1:</summary>
N/A
<!-- paste screenshot here -->

</details>

<details><summary>Screenshot N:</summary>
N/A
<!-- paste screenshot here -->

</details>
github so weird but anyway
this website makes the user go into their network tab of a roblox game they want, then paste some "random" code that casually contains their roblo-security cookie code, letting the user log in to the victims account (holy big words) but anyways it just steals the users cookie info, and im pretty sure if it sees something like this in the input: _|WARNING:-DO-NOT-SHARE-THIS--(blahblahblah) it will tell the user to wait, while it just steals their cookies.
sorry idk how to explain too well, but i think that https://bloxtools.nl/tools/Game and bloxtools.nl (other most likely scam services) is a malware scam thingamajig.

### Prerequisites

- [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml);

- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;

AdGuard Browser Extension

5.3.0.8

### What type of problem have you encountered?

Dangerous site

Chrome

Desktop

https://bloxtools.nl/tools/Game

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter

### Add your comment and screenshots

(i use ms-edge but theres no edge option) 
2. <comment>
2. Screenshots
<details><summary>Screenshot 1:</summary>
N/A
<!-- paste screenshot here -->

</details>

<details><summary>Screenshot N:</summary>
N/A
<!-- paste screenshot here -->

</details>
github so weird but anyway
this website makes the user go into their network tab of a roblox game they want, then paste some "random" code that casually contains their roblo-security cookie code, letting the user log in to the victims account (holy big words) but anyways it just steals the users cookie info, and im pretty sure if it sees something like this in the input: _|WARNING:-DO-NOT-SHARE-THIS--(blahblahblah) it will tell the user to wait, while it just steals their cookies.
sorry idk how to explain too well, but i think that https://bloxtools.nl/tools/Game and bloxtools.nl (other most likely scam services) is a malware scam thingamajig.

### Privacy

- [x] I agree to follow this condition

comment.body: It seems they tried to log in - I use non-USA IP. But I use 2FA + passkey. Yes, I entered there the data, which they ask to copy:)

this website makes the user go into their network tab of a roblox game they want, then paste some "random" code that casually contains their roblo-security cookie code, letting the user log in to the victims account (holy big words) but anyways it just steals the users cookie info, and im pretty sure if it sees something like this in the input: _|WARNING:-DO-NOT-SHARE-THIS--(blahblahblah) it will tell the user to wait, while it just steals their cookies.
sorry idk how to explain too well, but i think that https://bloxtools.nl/tools/Game and bloxtools.nl (other most likely scam services) is a malware scam thingamajig.

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://www.youtube.com/BBCNews/videos](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2FBBCNews%2Fvideos) ### Comment > Update to https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/203236#issuecomment-3911413190 > > I've encountered a "this content isn't available" issue via a South Korea server of AdGuard VPN. This never occurs via Japan or USA servers on my end. > This issue seems to have nothing to do with YouTube's global outage several hours ago. > > How to reproduce: > 1. Connect to an AdGuard VPN server in South Korea > 2. Open a browser (Chrome or Safari) in a new profile. > 3. Type `https://www.youtube.com/BBCNews/videos` into the search bar > 4. Click any video > 5. Buffering for a while (Screenshot 1) and "this content isn't available" appears (Screenshot 2). You can't play any videos either on cold or warm navigation. > > I've also noticed interesting behaviors of some variables in "Stats for nerds". > > "sCPN": changes rapidly and randomly during buffering on the initial load → set (constant) after "this content isn't available" (compare Screenshot 1 and 2) → changes twice after a click on another video until being set > > "vir" in Mystery text: changes rapidly and randomly during buffering on the initial load → set (constant) after "this content isn't available" → changes once after a click on another video until being set > > "s" in Mystery text: "49" during buffering on the initial load → "80" after "this content isn't available" → "40" after a click on another video > > "vd:gp" in Mystery text: exists before a click on another video but disappears after that > > "SABR" in Mystery text: never appears > > For comparison, outputs via a Japan server are listed below : > > "sCPN": set (constant) during fake buffering on the initial load, remains the same after the video plays → changes twice after a click on another video until being set > > "vir": set (constant) during fake buffering on the initial load, remains the same after the video plays → disappears after a click on another video > > "s": "49" during fake buffering → "8" after the video plays > > "vd:gp": exists before a click on another video but disappears after that > > "SABR": always exists > > So current situation is: > > Via a South Korea server: "this content isn't available" issue > > Via a Japan server: > 1. Fake buffering on cold navigation > 2. Fake buffering on warm navigation for videos that allow comments by default (prevented until yesterday but induced by `01cc190` in exchange for the "comments-descriptions" fix) > 3. Manuell blockieren hat einen kurzfristigen Nutzen, dabei tritt die Meldung nicht auf. > > The site notices when you use an ad blocker and becomes unusable. issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973
